title: fagraea obovata var. gardneri
intro: Fagraea obovata var. gardneri, also known as the fragrant frangipani, is a flowering plant species native to the tropical regions of Asia, particularly in Sri Lanka and India. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Fagraea obovata var. gardneri include its potential to treat various ailments such as fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as its ability to alleviate digestive problems and skin conditions. The plant can be prepared into herbal remedies like infusions, decoctions, and ointments to harness its medicinal properties, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption.
